titleStmt.title: Letter from the Commissioner of the R.I.C. to the Major Geoffrey Drage, 15 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the Commissioner of the Royal Irish Constabulary (R.I.C.) to the Chief Recruiting Office regarding David Max Cohen. According to the Commissioner Cohen is a Jew of Russian descent who has been residing in Ireland since the age of four. He is noted as a respectable man.Major Geoffrey Drage (1860-1955), politician, served from 1914 in the military intelligence section of the War Office. He was also the chief recruiting officer for Belfast.

correspAction.placeName: Commissioning Office, Royal Irish Constabulary, Belfast, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
15 July 1916.
Re David Max Cohen 27 Pacific Avenue
Now a British Subject
--------------***--------------- Sir, The Home Office having granted a Certificate
of Naturalization of the above-named,and,being under
41.Years of age I have been directed to communicate
with you in case you should desire to invite him to
enlist ortoattest. Cohen is a Jew, born of Russian parents and in this
country since he was a child of four years of age.
He is a respectable man. Your obedient servant
Commissioner
R.I.C. The Chief Recruiting Officer City Hall Belfast.
